description: Source-of-truth manifest for the Market Indicators tab. Every series has an expected title that the validator confirms against the live provider before scraping. chartId groups series that should render on the same chart panel; chartLabel is the legend label inside that chart. Unit codes map to formatters in web/src/indicator-chart.js: percent | dollar | dollar_millions | index | units | persons | ratio | balance_of_opinion.

mortgage_rates.title: Posted mortgage rates
mortgage_rates.description: Posted residential mortgage rates from chartered banks. Rising rates reduce buyer affordability and tighten investment-property cash flows, putting downward pressure on values; falling rates widen affordability, support price growth, and trigger refinancing activity. The variable line (broker average) often leads posted fixed rates by a few months.

goc_yields.title: Government of Canada bond yields
goc_yields.description: Benchmark Government of Canada bond yields. The 5-year is the canonical cap-rate anchor for multi-family and commercial appraisals — appraisers typically add 200–400 bps to derive an indicative cap rate. Rising yields push cap rates higher and depress property values; falling yields compress cap rates and support valuations.

policy_rates.title: Policy rate (overnight target)
policy_rates.description: Bank of Canada policy rate — the overnight target. Changes flow into variable mortgages within weeks and into posted fixed rates within months. Rising policy rates foreshadow softer transaction volumes; cuts often precede a rebound in activity. (CORRA was removed as a redundant indicator — it tracks the target rate within a few basis points by design.)

arrears.title: Mortgage arrears (90+ days)
arrears.description: Share of residential mortgages 90+ days in arrears. A leading indicator of distressed-sale supply: persistent rises typically precede increased forced sales, which appraisers should consider when bracketing comparable sales. Currently disabled — CBA data source is WAF-blocked; Phase 1.5 will source from CMHC or StatsCan.

slos_mortgage.title: SLOS — Mortgage lending conditions
slos_mortgage.description: Bank of Canada Senior Loan Officer Survey, balance-of-opinion index. Positive readings mean lenders are tightening (harder to qualify, lower LTV, stricter stress test); negative readings mean easing. Tightening foreshadows fewer marginal buyers and softer demand at the bottom of the market.

slos_business.title: SLOS — Business lending conditions
slos_business.description: Same survey but for commercial lending — critical for commercial appraisers. Positive readings indicate banks pulling back on CRE lending: deals stall, cap rates drift wider as buyers face tighter financing. Negative readings signal a healthier transaction environment with more comparables to choose from.

nhpi.title: New Housing Price Index
nhpi.description: Statistics Canada's New Housing Price Index — builder list prices for new SFD homes, excluding land. Rising NHPI indicates pricing power on new construction; broader resale market typically follows with a 3–6 month lag. Useful for time-adjusting comparable sales and as a feasibility check against rising construction costs.

nhpi_yoy.title: NHPI — year-over-year % change
nhpi_yoy.description: Annualised year-over-year change in NHPI. The 12-month growth rate is the standard time-adjustment factor appraisers apply to dated comparable sales: a sale 12 months ago + the YoY % moves the comparable to today's price level (subject to all the usual caveats about local versus index applicability).

cpi_shelter.title: CPI — Shelter component
cpi_shelter.description: Shelter component of the Consumer Price Index — captures rented + owned shelter inflation, including operating costs (utilities, insurance, property taxes). Rising values mean operating costs are climbing for landlords; flat nominal rents against rising shelter-CPI imply declining real rents. Useful for stabilising operating-expense assumptions in multi-family pro formas.

permits_residential.title: Residential building permits ($)
permits_residential.description: Seasonally-adjusted dollar value of residential building permits issued. A leading indicator of supply: surges in permits typically translate to completions 18–36 months out, putting downward pressure on rents and prices in that timeframe. Sustained low permit volumes signal scarcity ahead and support property values.

permits_nonresidential.title: Non-residential building permits ($)
permits_nonresidential.description: Seasonally-adjusted dollar value of industrial, commercial, and institutional permits. Tracks the commercial development pipeline — strong permits indicate investor confidence and incoming supply for office / retail / industrial space. Weak permits signal caution; useful context when stress-testing absorption assumptions in commercial appraisals.

population.title: Population estimates
population.description: Quarterly population estimates. The fundamental demand driver for residential markets — strong population growth supports rents, occupancy, and prices; flat or declining population signals demand weakness. Net interprovincial migration is the most volatile component for Manitoba.

unemployment_rate.title: Unemployment rate (15+, SA)
unemployment_rate.description: Seasonally-adjusted unemployment rate for the working-age population. A rising rate signals labour-market weakness that typically softens rental demand (renters lose jobs first) and slows the homebuyer pool. Falling rates indicate tight labour markets, supporting rent growth and price appreciation, though extreme tightness can flag overheating.

employment.title: Total employment (15+, SA)
employment.description: Total persons employed, seasonally adjusted. Useful as a job-creation count: positive year-over-year change indicates net hiring, which feeds household formation and rental absorption. For commercial appraisers, the trajectory in office-using industries is the key sub-signal — flagged for Phase 2b.

bcpi_residential.title: BCPI — Residential construction cost
bcpi_residential.description: Building Construction Price Index — residential composite, indexed to 2023=100. Captures contractor pricing for the full divisional bundle (concrete, framing, HVAC, finishes, etc.) on residential new construction. Rising BCPI directly raises the replacement-cost approach output and pressures developer margins; falling BCPI improves feasibility on new builds.

bcpi_residential_yoy.title: BCPI Residential — year-over-year % change
bcpi_residential_yoy.description: Annualised YoY change in residential BCPI. The going-rate of construction-cost inflation — directly feeds your replacement-cost approach update frequency, and tells you whether contractor pricing is accelerating or cooling. Useful when reviewing the inflation assumption baked into prior-year insurance-replacement updates.

bcpi_nonresidential.title: BCPI — Non-residential construction cost
bcpi_nonresidential.description: Building Construction Price Index — non-residential composite for office, retail, industrial, and institutional builds. The primary input to the replacement-cost approach for commercial appraisals. Track quarter-over-quarter movement when stress-testing depreciated replacement cost values; trended changes also feed insurance-replacement updates.

bcpi_nonresidential_yoy.title: BCPI Non-residential — year-over-year % change
bcpi_nonresidential_yoy.description: Annualised YoY change in non-residential BCPI. Same indicator as the residential YoY but for the office/retail/industrial/institutional cost bundle — the most direct signal of whether commercial replacement-cost trended numbers are still defensible at last year's depreciation assumption.

cap_rate_pressure.title: Cap-rate pressure (5-yr GoC + spread)
cap_rate_pressure.description: The 5-year Government of Canada bond yield with 200 / 300 / 400 bps overlays — a rough scaffolding for the cap-rate range an institutional buyer would underwrite. Pick the spread that fits your asset class (multi-family closer to 200-250, well-located commercial 300, secondary commercial 350+). Use these as a first sanity check on cap-rate inputs in income-approach work; replace with deal-specific spreads when comparables exist.

feasibility.title: Construction feasibility — NHPI ÷ BCPI
feasibility.description: Ratio of new-home prices (NHPI) to residential construction cost (BCPI), with both indexed so the ratio is interpretable as relative momentum. Rising means new-home prices are outpacing construction costs — feasibility improving for developers. Falling means costs are squeezing margins; expect builders to slow starts or push for price increases. Caveat: this is a national/CMA composite — local land cost and the lot premium are not captured.
